Data Driven - Project

Nikita, Trilok, Deekshita

2024-10-10

Reading the Dataset

# Read the CSV file
HOU <- read.csv("C:/Users/dsrin/OneDrive/Desktop/MSBA/Data Driven/Data Driven Project/OUData.csv")
# View the first few rows of the dataset
head(HOU)
##   ObservationRecordKey Age Gender PrimaryInsuranceCategory

Understanding the Dataset

# Structure of the data
str(HOU)
## 'data.frame':    1111 obs. of  15 variables:
##  $ ObservationRecordKey               : chr  "905459x1" "443621z2" "131565z1" "438080x1" ...
##  $ Age                                : int  54 89 83 89 81 54 83 50 52 85 ...
##  $ Gender                             : chr  "Male" "Female" "Female" "Female" ...
##  $ PrimaryInsuranceCategory           : chr  "MEDICAID STATE" "MEDICARE" "MEDICARE" "MEDICARE" ...
##  $ InitPatientClassAndFirstPostOUClass: chr  "OBSERVATION->INPATIENT" "OBSERVATION->OBSERVATION" "OBSERVATION->INPATIENT" "OBSERVATION->OBSERVATION" ...
##  $ Flipped                            : int  1 0 1 0 0 0 1 1 0 1 ...
##  $ OU_LOS_hrs                         : num  37.3 89.7 96.3 13.3 25.4 ...
##  $ DRG01                              : int  428 599 786 780 428 786 558 780 786 780 ...
##  $ BloodPressureUpper                 : chr  "153" "123" "105" "162" ...
##  $ BloodPressureLower                 : int  111 64 55 73 60 56 61 48 85 60 ...
##  $ BloodPressureDiff                  : chr  "26" "68" "29" "83" ...
##  $ Pulse                              : chr  "73" "86" "81" "76" ...
##  $ PulseOximetry                      : chr  "100" "94" "94" "97" ...
##  $ Respirations                       : chr  "18" "18" "18" "24" ...
##  $ Temperature                        : chr  "98.2" "95.9" "97.3" "98.1" ...
# Summary Statistics of the data
summary(HOU)

dim(HOU)

Changing the Data types to numeric

#Setting the all the numeric variables as numeric
HOU$BloodPressureUpper <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$BloodPressureUpper))
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
HOU$BloodPressureLower <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$BloodPressureLower))

HOU$BloodPressureDiff <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$BloodPressureDiff))
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
HOU$Pulse <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$Pulse))
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
HOU$PulseOximetry <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$PulseOximetry))
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
HOU$Respirations <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$Respirations))
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
HOU$OU_LOS_hrs <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$OU_LOS_hrs))

HOU$Temperature <- as.numeric(as.character(HOU$Temperature))
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
#Factoring so as to represent the related groups
HOU$DRG01 <- factor(HOU$DRG01, 
                      levels=c(276, 428, 486, 558, 577, 578, 599, 780, 782, 786, 787, 789),
                      labels = c("Dehydration", "Congestive Heart Failure", "Pneumonia", "Colitis", "Pancreatitis", "GI Bleeding", "Urinary Tract Infection", "Syncope", "Edema", "Chest pain", "Nausea", "Abdominal pain"))

Exploratory Data Analysis

#Checking for missing values and displaying
Missing = sum(is.na(HOU))
Missing
## [1] 20
# Mean Imputation for Numeric Columns
HOU_imputed <- HOU  # Make a copy of the data

numeric_vars <- sapply(HOU_imputed, is.numeric)  # Identify numeric columns
HOU_imputed[numeric_vars] <- lapply(HOU_imputed[numeric_vars], function(x) {
  ifelse(is.na(x), mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), x)
})
#Checking for missing values and displaying
Missing = sum(is.na(HOU_imputed))
Missing
## [1] 0
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.2.3
library(dplyr)
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.2.3
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(reshape2)  # For reshaping the correlation matrix for ggplot
## Warning: package 'reshape2' was built under R version 4.2.3
# Select only numeric columns from the original dataset
numeric_data_with_outliers <- HOU %>%
  select_if(is.numeric)

# Calculate the correlation matrix
correlation_matrix_with_outliers <- cor(numeric_data_with_outliers, use = "complete.obs")

# Melt the correlation matrix into long format for ggplot
correlation_data_with_outliers <- melt(correlation_matrix_with_outliers)

# Create the heatmap
ggplot(data = correlation_data_with_outliers, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white",
                       midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1), space = "Lab",
                       name = "Correlation") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Correlation Heatmap of Numeric Variables (With Outliers)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank())

# Load necessary library
library(ggplot2)

# List of numeric variables to plot
numeric_vars <- c("Pulse", "BloodPressureUpper", "BloodPressureLower", "Temperature", "Respirations", 
                  "Age", "OU_LOS_hrs", "PulseOximetry", "BloodPressureDiff")

# Loop through each variable and create a box plot
for (var in numeric_vars) {
  print(
    ggplot(HOU, aes_string(y = var)) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
      labs(title = paste("Boxplot of", var), y = var) +
      theme_minimal()
  )
}

## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

## (`stat_boxplot()`).

## Warning: Removed 3 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

## Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_boxplot()`).

# Function to remove outliers based on IQR for a given column
remove_outliers <- function(data, column) {
  Q1 <- quantile(data[[column]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  Q3 <- quantile(data[[column]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  IQR <- Q3 - Q1
  lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
  upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
  data %>% filter(data[[column]] >= lower_bound & data[[column]] <= upper_bound)
}

# List of numeric variables for outlier removal
numeric_vars <- c("Pulse", "BloodPressureUpper", "BloodPressureLower", "Temperature", "Respirations", 
                  "Age", "OU_LOS_hrs", "PulseOximetry", "BloodPressureDiff", "Flipped")

# Apply outlier removal for each variable in the dataset
HOU_no_outliers <- HOU  # Start with the original dataset

for (var in numeric_vars) {
  HOU_no_outliers <- remove_outliers(HOU_no_outliers, var)
}

# Check the dimensions of the dataset after removing outliers
dim(HOU_no_outliers)
## [1] 826  15
# Load necessary library
library(ggplot2)

# List of numeric variables to plot
numeric_vars <- c("Pulse", "BloodPressureUpper", "BloodPressureLower", "Temperature", "Respirations", 
                  "Age", "OU_LOS_hrs", "PulseOximetry", "BloodPressureDiff")

# Loop through each variable and create a box plot
for (var in numeric_vars) {
  print(
    ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes_string(y = var)) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +
      labs(title = paste("Boxplot of", var, "(Outliers Removed)"), y = var) +
      theme_minimal()
  )
}

ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes(x = Pulse)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Pulse", x = "Pulse", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes(x = Age, y = OU_LOS_hrs)) +
  geom_bin2d() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "skyblue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Heatmap of Age vs Length of Stay", x = "Age", y = "Length of Stay (hrs)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Load necessary library
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes(x = Pulse, y = OU_LOS_hrs)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  facet_wrap(~ DRG01) + 
  labs(title = "Pulse vs Length of Stay by Diagnosis (Faceted)", 
       x = "Pulse", y = "Length of Stay (hours)")

# Load necessary library
library(dplyr)

# Create age groups
HOU_no_outliers <- HOU_no_outliers %>%
  mutate(AgeGroup = case_when(
    Age < 18 ~ "Child",
    Age >= 18 & Age < 35 ~ "Young Adult",
    Age >= 35 & Age < 55 ~ "Adult",
    Age >= 55 & Age < 75 ~ "Senior",
    Age >= 75 ~ "Elderly"
  ))
# Load ggplot2 library
library(ggplot2)

# Box plot of Length of Stay by Age Group
ggplot(HOU_no_outliers, aes(x = AgeGroup, y = OU_LOS_hrs)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Length of Stay by Age Group", x = "Age Group", y = "Length of Stay (hrs)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Perform one-way ANOVA
anova_result <- aov(OU_LOS_hrs ~ DRG01, data = HOU_no_outliers)

# Summary of the ANOVA results
summary(anova_result)
##              Df  Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
## DRG01        11   89192    8108    5.95 2.25e-09 ***
## Residuals   814 1109226    1363                     
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# Tukey's HSD test for pairwise comparisons
tukey_result <- TukeyHSD(anova_result)

# Summary of Tukey's test results
print(tukey_result)
##   Tukey multiple comparisons of means
##     95% family-wise confidence level
## 
## Fit: aov(formula = OU_LOS_hrs ~ DRG01, data = HOU_no_outliers)
##

# Perform one-way ANOVA
anova_result_age <- aov(OU_LOS_hrs ~ AgeGroup, data = HOU_no_outliers)

# Summary of the ANOVA results
summary(anova_result_age)
##              Df  Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
## AgeGroup      3   57654   19218   13.85 8.14e-09 ***
## Residuals   822 1140764    1388                     
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# Tukey's HSD test for pairwise comparisons
tukey_result_age <- TukeyHSD(anova_result_age)

# Summary of Tukey's test results
print(tukey_result_age)
##   Tukey multiple comparisons of means
##     95% family-wise confidence level
## 
## Fit: aov(formula = OU_LOS_hrs ~ AgeGroup, data = HOU_no_outliers)
## 
## $AgeGroup
##                           diff        lwr       upr     p adj
## Elderly-Adult        16.372882   6.933758 25.812006 0.0000538
## Senior-Adult          1.301647  -8.746979 11.350272 0.9872138
## Young Adult-Adult    -6.470098 -22.014539  9.074344 0.7070313
## Senior-Elderly      -15.071235 -22.851885 -7.290585 0.0000045
## Young Adult-Elderly -22.842979 -37.027241 -8.658717 0.0002185
## Young Adult-Senior   -7.771744 -22.368700  6.825211 0.5182083
# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# Define the training index (random sample of 70% of rows)
train_index <- sample(seq_len(nrow(HOU_no_outliers)), size = 0.7 * nrow(HOU_no_outliers))

# Split the dataset
train_data <- HOU_no_outliers[train_index, ]  # 70% for training
test_data <- HOU_no_outliers[-train_index, ]  # 30% for testing

# Check dimensions to confirm the split
cat("Training Set:", dim(train_data), "\n")
## Training Set: 578 16
cat("Testing Set:", dim(test_data), "\n")
## Testing Set: 248 16
# Run a logistic regression model
logistic_model <- glm(Flipped ~ Age + Pulse + BloodPressureUpper + PulseOximetry + BloodPressureLower + Temperature + Respirations + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01,
                      data = train_data, 
                      family = binomial)

# Summary of the logistic regression model
summary(logistic_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = Flipped ~ Age + Pulse + BloodPressureUpper + PulseOximetry + 
##     BloodPressureLower + Temperature + Respirations + OU_LOS_hrs + 
##     DRG01, family = binomial, data = train_data)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  
## -3.0134  -0.6838  -0.3194   0.6850   2.7083  
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                   -2.705e+01  1.451e+01  -1.864   0.0623 .  
## Age                            7.629e-04  6.701e-03   0.114   0.9094    
## Pulse                          1.075e-02  7.691e-03   1.398   0.1620    
## BloodPressureUpper             3.363e-03  5.526e-03   0.609   0.5428    
## PulseOximetry                  5.840e-02  4.745e-02   1.231   0.2184    
## BloodPressureLower            -2.085e-02  1.035e-02  -2.015   0.0439 *  
## Temperature                    2.073e-01  1.351e-01   1.534   0.1251    
## Respirations                  -5.283e-02  5.883e-02  -0.898   0.3692    
## OU_LOS_hrs                     5.131e-02  4.802e-03  10.684  < 2e-16 ***
## DRG01Congestive Heart Failure  4.635e-01  5.751e-01   0.806   0.4203    
## DRG01Pneumonia                 1.421e-01  5.524e-01   0.257   0.7970    
## DRG01Colitis                   1.258e+00  7.635e-01   1.648   0.0994 .  
## DRG01Pancreatitis              3.970e-01  7.653e-01   0.519   0.6039    
## DRG01GI Bleeding              -2.122e-01  5.492e-01  -0.386   0.6992    
## DRG01Urinary Tract Infection  -1.480e-01  5.392e-01  -0.274   0.7838    
## DRG01Syncope                  -1.903e+00  4.147e-01  -4.589 4.46e-06 ***
## DRG01Edema                     3.726e-01  7.917e-01   0.471   0.6379    
## DRG01Chest pain               -8.688e-01  4.437e-01  -1.958   0.0502 .  
## DRG01Nausea                   -8.156e-01  5.032e-01  -1.621   0.1050    
## DRG01Abdominal pain           -8.956e-01  4.582e-01  -1.955   0.0506 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 797.29  on 577  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 517.11  on 558  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 557.11
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 5
stepwise_model <- step(logistic_model, direction = "both")
## Start:  AIC=557.11
## Flipped ~ Age + Pulse + BloodPressureUpper + PulseOximetry + 
##     BloodPressureLower + Temperature + Respirations + OU_LOS_hrs + 
##     DRG01
## 
##                      Df Deviance    AIC
## - Age                 1   517.12 555.12
## - BloodPressureUpper  1   517.48 555.48
## - Respirations        1   517.92 555.92
## - PulseOximetry       1   518.64 556.64
## - Pulse               1   519.07 557.07
## <none>                    517.11 557.11
## - Temperature         1   519.48 557.48
## - BloodPressureLower  1   521.22 559.22
## - DRG01              11   576.59 594.59
## - OU_LOS_hrs          1   734.01 772.01
## 
## Step:  AIC=555.12
## Flipped ~ Pulse + BloodPressureUpper + PulseOximetry + BloodPressureLower + 
##     Temperature + Respirations + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01
## 
##                      Df Deviance    AIC
## - BloodPressureUpper  1   517.51 553.51
## - Respirations        1   517.93 553.93
## - PulseOximetry       1   518.65 554.65
## - Pulse               1   519.08 555.08
## <none>                    517.12 555.12
## - Temperature         1   519.48 555.48
## + Age                 1   517.11 557.11
## - BloodPressureLower  1   521.23 557.23
## - DRG01              11   577.05 593.05
## - OU_LOS_hrs          1   738.79 774.79
## 
## Step:  AIC=553.51
## Flipped ~ Pulse + PulseOximetry + BloodPressureLower + Temperature + 
##     Respirations + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01
## 
##                      Df Deviance    AIC
## - Respirations        1   518.23 552.23
## - PulseOximetry       1   518.90 552.90
## - Pulse               1   519.24 553.24
## <none>                    517.51 553.51
## - Temperature         1   519.93 553.93
## + BloodPressureUpper  1   517.12 555.12
## + Age                 1   517.48 555.48
## - BloodPressureLower  1   521.59 555.59
## - DRG01              11   577.33 591.33
## - OU_LOS_hrs          1   740.17 774.17
## 
## Step:  AIC=552.23
## Flipped ~ Pulse + PulseOximetry + BloodPressureLower + Temperature + 
##     OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01
## 
##                      Df Deviance    AIC
## - Pulse               1   519.74 551.74
## - PulseOximetry       1   519.94 551.94
## <none>                    518.23 552.23
## - Temperature         1   520.57 552.57
## + Respirations        1   517.51 553.51
## + BloodPressureUpper  1   517.93 553.93
## + Age                 1   518.21 554.21
## - BloodPressureLower  1   522.70 554.70
## - DRG01              11   577.59 589.59
## - OU_LOS_hrs          1   740.51 772.51
## 
## Step:  AIC=551.74
## Flipped ~ PulseOximetry + BloodPressureLower + Temperature + 
##     OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01
## 
##                      Df Deviance    AIC
## - PulseOximetry       1   521.12 551.12
## <none>                    519.74 551.74
## + Pulse               1   518.23 552.23
## - Temperature         1   522.52 552.52
## + Respirations        1   519.24 553.24
## - BloodPressureLower  1   523.48 553.48
## + BloodPressureUpper  1   519.61 553.61
## + Age                 1   519.74 553.74
## - DRG01              11   580.80 590.80
## - OU_LOS_hrs          1   740.55 770.55
## 
## Step:  AIC=551.12
## Flipped ~ BloodPressureLower + Temperature + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01
## 
##                      Df Deviance    AIC
## <none>                    521.12 551.12
## - Temperature         1   523.48 551.48
## + PulseOximetry       1   519.74 551.74
## + Pulse               1   519.94 551.94
## - BloodPressureLower  1   524.25 552.25
## + Respirations        1   520.35 552.35
## + BloodPressureUpper  1   521.06 553.06
## + Age                 1   521.10 553.10
## - DRG01              11   582.90 590.90
## - OU_LOS_hrs          1   740.92 768.92
# Run the updated logistic regression model with significant variables
updated_logistic_model <- glm(Flipped ~ BloodPressureLower + Temperature + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01,
                              data = train_data, 
                              family = binomial)

# Summary of the updated logistic regression model
summary(updated_logistic_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = Flipped ~ BloodPressureLower + Temperature + OU_LOS_hrs + 
##     DRG01, family = binomial, data = train_data)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  
## -2.9376  -0.7031  -0.3251   0.7042   2.6504  
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                   -21.060231  13.016988  -1.618   0.1057    
## BloodPressureLower             -0.014617   0.008285  -1.764   0.0777 .  
## Temperature                     0.204036   0.133064   1.533   0.1252    
## OU_LOS_hrs                      0.050634   0.004690  10.795  < 2e-16 ***
## DRG01Congestive Heart Failure   0.471135   0.566990   0.831   0.4060    
## DRG01Pneumonia                  0.076229   0.544326   0.140   0.8886    
## DRG01Colitis                    1.212383   0.753004   1.610   0.1074    
## DRG01Pancreatitis               0.370086   0.765235   0.484   0.6287    
## DRG01GI Bleeding               -0.147751   0.541466  -0.273   0.7850    
## DRG01Urinary Tract Infection   -0.055473   0.532821  -0.104   0.9171    
## DRG01Syncope                   -1.901634   0.407857  -4.663 3.12e-06 ***
## DRG01Edema                      0.527529   0.781938   0.675   0.4999    
## DRG01Chest pain                -0.916217   0.438232  -2.091   0.0366 *  
## DRG01Nausea                    -0.821802   0.498026  -1.650   0.0989 .  
## DRG01Abdominal pain            -0.915065   0.440199  -2.079   0.0376 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 797.29  on 577  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 521.12  on 563  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 551.12
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 5
# Load necessary libraries
library(pROC)
## Warning: package 'pROC' was built under R version 4.2.3
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
library(ggplot2)

# Predict probabilities on the test data using the updated logistic model
test_data$predicted_probs <- predict(updated_logistic_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")

# Generate the ROC curve
roc_curve <- roc(test_data$Flipped, test_data$predicted_probs)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
# Plot the ROC curve using ggplot2
ggplot(data = as.data.frame(roc_curve$sensitivities), aes(x = 1 - roc_curve$specificities, y = roc_curve$sensitivities)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  geom_abline(linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + # Diagonal line for random guessing
  labs(title = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression Model",
       x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
       y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.25, label = paste("AUC =", round(auc(roc_curve), 2)), color = "black")

# Predict probabilities of Flipped = 1
predictions <- predict(updated_logistic_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")

# Convert probabilities to binary outcomes (0 or 1) using a threshold (e.g., 0.5)
predicted_class <- ifelse(predictions > 0.5, 1, 0)
# Confusion matrix
table(Predicted = predicted_class, Actual = test_data$Flipped)
##          Actual
## Predicted   0   1
##         0 116  32
##         1  31  69
# Alternatively, calculate accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity
library(caret)
## Warning: package 'caret' was built under R version 4.2.3
## Loading required package: lattice
confusionMatrix(as.factor(predicted_class), as.factor(test_data$Flipped))
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   0   1
##          0 116  32
##          1  31  69
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.746          
##                  95% CI : (0.687, 0.7989)
##     No Information Rate : 0.5927         
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 3.105e-07      
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.473          
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 1              
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 0.7891         
##             Specificity : 0.6832         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.7838         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.6900         
##              Prevalence : 0.5927         
##          Detection Rate : 0.4677         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.5968         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.7361         
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : 0              
## 
train_data$Flipped <- as.factor(train_data$Flipped)
test_data$Flipped <- as.factor(test_data$Flipped)
library(randomForest)
## Warning: package 'randomForest' was built under R version 4.2.3
## randomForest 4.7-1.1
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.
## 
## Attaching package: 'randomForest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     combine
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     margin
rf_model <- randomForest(Flipped ~ BloodPressureLower + Temperature + OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01, 
                         data = train_data, 
                         ntree = 500, 
                         mtry = 2, 
                         importance = TRUE)
print(rf_model)
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = Flipped ~ BloodPressureLower + Temperature +      OU_LOS_hrs + DRG01, data = train_data, ntree = 500, mtry = 2,      importance = TRUE) 
##                Type of random forest: classification
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 2
## 
##         OOB estimate of  error rate: 23.36%
## Confusion matrix:
##     0   1 class.error
## 0 249  64   0.2044728
## 1  71 194   0.2679245
varImpPlot(rf_model, type = 2)

# Get prediction probabilities for the positive class
pred_probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")[, 2]

# Generate the ROC curve
library(pROC)
roc_curve <- roc(test_data$Flipped, pred_probs)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_curve, main = "ROC Curve for Random Forest Model")

library(caret)

# Predict probabilities and convert to classes with threshold of 0.5
pred_probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")[, 2]
pred_classes <- ifelse(pred_probs > 0.5, "1", "0")
pred_classes <- as.factor(pred_classes)

# Ensure that the levels of prediction and actual data are consistent
levels(pred_classes) <- levels(test_data$Flipped)

# Generate and print the confusion matrix
confusion_matrix <- confusionMatrix(pred_classes, test_data$Flipped, positive = "1")
print(confusion_matrix)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   0   1
##          0 112  27
##          1  35  74
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.75            
##                  95% CI : (0.6913, 0.8026)
##     No Information Rate : 0.5927          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1.502e-07       
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.4885          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.374           
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.7327          
##             Specificity : 0.7619          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.6789          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.8058          
##              Prevalence : 0.4073          
##          Detection Rate : 0.2984          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.4395          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.7473          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : 1               
##
